name: Rural/Suburban Transition
milkyWayVisibility: Good — Milky Way still obvious but losing detail; light domes prominent in several directions
visibleObjects: Light pollution domes in multiple directions
nearestDarkSkyReserves: Exmoor National Park Dark Sky Reserve
description: Transitional skies between rural and suburban environments. The Milky Way is visible overhead but is losing fine detail and is notably degraded near the horizon. Light pollution domes from nearby towns and cities are visible in multiple directions. The zodiacal light is barely detectable. This is the threshold where untrained observers begin to notice the night sky is not fully dark. Common in exurban areas, large villages, and the outskirts of small cities. These skies represent the best conditions available to many amateur astronomers.
